title: API Resources, Object Identity, Schemas, Desired State, and Status · Kubernetes Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release review compares fresh api resources, object identity, schemas, desired state, and status choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned API object contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve API group, version, kind, plural resource, scope, endpoint, and served or stored version for a concrete object.
Separate desired spec, controller-owned status, metadata generation, observedGeneration, and status conditions before judging convergence.
Use namespace, name, UID, resourceVersion, generation, timestamps, and managed fields to distinguish identity, concurrency, and history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
